title: Avoid using boxed Boolean types directly in boolean expressions
description: When boxed type java.lang.Boolean is used as an expression to determine the control flow, it will throw a NullPointerException if the value is null. It is safer to avoid such conversion altogether and handle the null value explicitly.
recommendation: Avoid using boxed Boolean types directly in boolean expressions. Handle the null value explicitly.

checks.message: Avoid using boxed Boolean types directly in boolean expressions. Handle the null value explicitly.
